data.title: LM Funding America outlines immersion mining expansion and $15.5M Bitcoin holdings value amid operational efficiency drive
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: LM Funding America (LMFA) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Bruce Rodgers, CEO, stated that "The first quarter of 2025 marked another period of strong execution and strategic progress for LM Funding. Since entering the Bitcoin mining business in 2021, we have transitioned from an asset-like model to a vertically integrated operator, gaining full control of our fleet, improving margins, and reducing operational risks." He highlighted the mining of 24.3 Bitcoin in the quarter and the company's move to monetize curtailment energy sales, selling energy back to the grid to hedge against Bitcoin price volatility and reduce operational costs.
  • Rodgers emphasized a disciplined approach to cost management, noting progress in margin improvement and cost control. He drew attention to the company's Bitcoin holdings, stating, "On April 30th, we held 148.7 Bitcoin. Given a market price of $104,000 per Bitcoin, our Bitcoin holdings would be worth $15.5 million or approximately $3.01 per share, when our stock is trading at $1.87 as of May 13th close."
  • Ryan Duran, President of U.S. Digital Mining, reported a focus on maximizing operational efficiency, achieving 560 petahash at March-end, and deploying LuxOS upgrades across the Oklahoma fleet. The company executed its first power sales back to the grid and ordered two 1-megawatt immersion mining containers for a 2-megawatt Oklahoma site expansion, expected to complete by the end of Q3 2025. Duran revealed a strategic shift to immersion cooling, which led to the sale of 256 Bitmain S21+ machines, anticipating the transaction to be cash-neutral, and the relocation of 800 machines to the company's Oklahoma site for lower power costs.
  • Richard Russell, CFO, stated, "In the first quarter of 2025, we mined 24.3 Bitcoins at an average price of $93,500, which was 12% more Bitcoins mined sequentially. Bitcoin mining revenue for the quarter was approximately $2.3 million, up 25% sequentially, reflecting the success of our infrastructure investments and improved machine efficiency from the LuxOS upgrades." Russell added that the company generated approximately $150,000 from power sales back to the grid and improved mining margins from 31.2% in Q4 2024 to 38.5% in Q1 2025. He also noted a 7.7% year-over-year reduction in staff costs, professional fees, SG&A, and other costs.

Outlook

  • Rodgers expressed excitement about beginning immersion mining at the Oklahoma site, expecting "immediate returns" and the potential to expand immersion technology to additional Greenfield and Brownfield sites in the 5MW to 20MW range. He stated the company's goal of building a sustainable business model around sites that can thrive in various market conditions, combining mining and energy sales.
  • The company maintains a long-term Bitcoin accumulation strategy, with Rodgers affirming, "Holding and mining Bitcoin remains a logical continuation of this strategy."
  • Management highlighted the possibility of adding Bitcoin to the balance sheet with debt and/or equity, referencing a $5 million loan secured by Bitcoin holdings in 2024.

Financial Results

  • Russell reported a net loss for the quarter of $5.4 million and a core EBITDA loss of $2.8 million, both impacted by a $1.8 million non-cash Bitcoin write-down as of March 31, 2025. He indicated that this write-down has nearly reversed due to Bitcoin price recovery.
  • The company ended the quarter with $1 million in cash and 160.2 Bitcoin valued at $13.2 million as of March 31, 2025. As of April 30, holdings stood at 148.7 Bitcoin, with a calculated value of $15.5 million at recent prices.

Q&A

  • Michael Donovan, H.C. Wainwright: Questioned details on the Oklahoma site build-out and expansion plans in Texas. Rodgers responded that Texas faces a counterpart issue and no immediate action is planned, while Oklahoma expansion is on schedule.
  • Donovan asked if the sale of S21 machines resolved inventory concerns. Rodgers replied, "Taking care of it, I don't know what that means. So, we did sell S21’s and [Technical Difficulty]."
  • Donovan inquired about geographic targets for new sites in the 5MW to 20MW range. Rodgers indicated ongoing business in Oklahoma and continued exploration, but specifics were unclear due to technical difficulties.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral, focused on clarifying operational details, site expansion, and inventory management without evident skepticism or pressing follow-up.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing operational achievements and strategic progress. In Q&A, some ambiguity and technical difficulties led to less direct answers, but no defensiveness was evident.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's confidence remained consistent, with continued emphasis on vertical integration and operational efficiency. Analysts' sentiment also remained neutral, with questions centered on execution and expansion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company increased its mined Bitcoin sequentially and improved mining margins from 31.2% in Q4 2024 to 38.5% in Q1 2025, driven by infrastructure upgrades and cost management.
  • The previous quarter highlighted profitability on a core-EBITDA basis and a stronger net income due to gains on Bitcoin fair value, while the current quarter saw a net loss due to a non-cash write-down, nearly reversed by price recovery.
  • Strategic focus shifted to immersion cooling, monetizing curtailment energy, and continued vertical integration, while geographic expansion into Texas slowed due to counterpart issues.
  • Analysts' focus remained on operational execution, hardware deployment, and site expansion, with consistent tone across both quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified Bitcoin price volatility as a core risk and is mitigating this through energy curtailment sales and operational flexibility.
  • The company faces challenges related to machine deployment, inventory management, and dependence on timely infrastructure development, particularly regarding shipping and site construction timelines.
  • Analyst concerns centered on site build-out progress, hardware deployment, and geographic expansion uncertainty.

Final Takeaway

LM Funding America is advancing its vertically integrated mining model, increasing operational efficiency, and accelerating the adoption of immersion cooling technology at its Oklahoma site. Strategic efforts have led to improved mining margins and cost reductions, while the company's Bitcoin holdings significantly exceed its market capitalization. Management remains committed to its long-term Bitcoin accumulation and operational expansion strategy, leveraging energy sales as a hedge against market volatility and seeking new site opportunities to drive shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Coinbase estimates up to $400M in customer payouts after data breach
data.description:
Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) expects to pay $180M-$400M in reimbursements and remediation after a recent data breach exposed sensitive customer information and led to users being tricked into sending funds to the attackers, it said on Thursday.

On May 11, the crypto exchange "received an email communication from an unknown threat actor claiming to have obtained information about certain Coinbase customer accounts, as well as internal Coinbase documentation, including materials relating to customer-service and account-management systems," it disclosed in an SEC filing.

The attackers allegedly paid overseas support staff to gain internal access and gather customer data. Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) noted it has already flagged and fired those involved.

From there, the cyber criminals demanded $20M to keep the stolen information private, though Coinbase (COIN) rejected the extortion attempt and instead put up a $20M reward for information leading to their arrest and conviction, according to a blog post.

In addition to the reward fund, the company has tagged the attackers' addresses so that the authorities can trace the stolen funds and work to recover stolen assets.

The scammers did not access login credentials, private keys, any ability to move or access customer funds, access to Coinbase Prime accounts, and access to any Coinbase or Coinbase customer hot or cold wallets.

COIN shares gapped down 7.6% in late morning trading.

data.title: Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust press release (OTC:APPTF): Q1 AFFO per Unit of $0.247 (diluted) and paid regular cash distributions of $0.201 per Unit.
  • Rental revenue in Q1 2025 increased by 2.1% to $23.9 million, compared to $23.4 million in Q1 2024. 
  • Net income and other comprehensive income of $7.6 million in Q1 2025, compared to $20.9 million in Q1 2024. 
  • FFO in Q1 2025 increased 4.1% to $12.6 million, or $0.251 per Unit.
  • AFFO in Q1 2025 increased 6.0% to $12.4 million, or $0.247 per Unit.
